title: Bloom, B. S. (1984). The 2 Sigma Problem: The Search for Methods of Group Instruction as Effective as One-to-One Tutoring
relevance: Feature: density of formative-check -> correction -> re-test cycles per concept in a transcript as a mastery-orientation proxy; sets the 2-sigma ceiling for tutored signal.

title: VanLehn, K. (2011). The Relative Effectiveness of Human Tutoring, Intelligent Tutoring Systems, and Other Tutoring Systems
relevance: Modeling: encode interaction granularity (answer- vs step- vs substep-level exchanges) as features; step-level feedback drives most of the gain (interaction plateau).

title: Nickow, Oreopoulos & Quan (2020). The Impressive Effects of Tutoring on PreK-12 Learning: A Meta-Analysis (NBER w27476)
relevance: Feature: dosage/intensity proxies (turns, session length, on-task frequency) calibrated against the ~0.29-0.30 SD effect prior; strongest moderators = tutor type, frequency, in-school.

title: Kulik & Fletcher (2016). Effectiveness of Intelligent Tutoring Systems: A Meta-Analytic Review
relevance: Modeling: use identified moderators (feedback immediacy, mastery gating, hint quality) as engineered features; provides expected effect-size priors for regularization.

title: Ma, Adesope, Nesbit & Liu (2014). Intelligent Tutoring Systems and Learning Outcomes: A Meta-Analysis
relevance: Modeling: evidence that ITS ~= individual human tutoring justifies building tutor-agnostic transcript features that generalize across tutoring modality.

title: Do intelligent tutoring systems benefit K-12 students? A meta-analysis and heterogeneity of treatment effects in the U.S. (2025)
relevance: Modeling: heterogeneity-of-treatment-effects framing motivates adding student-subgroup interaction terms to the correctness predictor rather than a single average signal.

title: Chi & Wylie (2014). The ICAP Framework: Linking Cognitive Engagement to Active Learning Outcomes
relevance: Feature: label each student turn Passive/Active/Constructive/Interactive; a higher constructive+interactive ratio should predict learning and next-item correctness.

title: Chi, De Leeuw, Chiu & LaVancher (1994). Eliciting Self-Explanations Improves Understanding
relevance: Feature: count/length/quality of student-generated self-explanations (beyond bare answers) as a high-value predictor of deep understanding.

title: Wisniewski, Zierer & Hattie (2020). The Power of Feedback Revisited: A Meta-Analysis of Educational Feedback Research
relevance: Feature: classify tutor feedback by level (task/process/self-regulation) and information content; high-information process feedback predicts larger gains (d~0.48 but heterogeneous).

title: D'Mello & Graesser. Confusion / Dynamics of Affective States During Complex Learning
relevance: Feature: detect confusion/frustration/boredom markers and confusion->resolution transitions; resolved (regulated) confusion is the affect that predicts learning.

title: NSW CESE (2017). Cognitive Load Theory: Research That Teachers Really Need to Understand (Sweller worked-example effect)
relevance: Feature: ratio of worked-example study vs unsupported problem-solving turns and example-before-problem sequencing, especially for novice (low-prior) students.

title: Corbett & Anderson (1994). Knowledge Tracing: Modeling the Acquisition of Procedural Knowledge (BKT)
relevance: Modeling: maintain a latent per-skill mastery probability updated across the session as the core state feeding P(next answer correct).

title: Piech et al. (2015). Deep Knowledge Tracing
relevance: Modeling: RNN/LSTM over the interaction sequence outputting P(next correct) is the direct backbone; extend inputs with transcript-derived features.

title: Abdelrahman, Wang & Nunes (2022). Knowledge Tracing: A Survey
relevance: Modeling: menu of KT architectures and auxiliary features (forgetting, item difficulty, attention) to adapt for a transcript-conditioned next-correct predictor.

title: Galyardt & Goldin (2015). Predicting Performance During Tutoring with Models of Recent Performance
relevance: Feature: recent-window correctness / streak / recency-weighted features often beat full-history models — cheap, tutor-agnostic signal for next-item correctness.

title: Decision Tree Modeling of Wheel-Spinning and Productive Persistence in Skill Builders (Beck/Gong lineage, JEDM)
relevance: Feature: repeated attempts on one skill without three-in-a-row correct (wheel-spinning) as a strong negative predictor of next-correct and future mastery.

title: Baker, Corbett & Koedinger (2004). Detecting Student Misuse of Intelligent Tutoring Systems (gaming the system)
relevance: Feature: rapid-fire attempts, systematic guessing, hint/help abuse and very low response latency as gaming markers that depress next-correct probability.

title: Aleven et al. (2016). Help Seeking and Intelligent Tutoring Systems: Toward Theoretical Integration
relevance: Feature: instrumental vs executive help-seeking (hint-level drill-down, bottom-out abuse, help avoidance) as metacognitive predictors of learning and correctness.

title: Understanding Student Effort Using Response-Time Propensities During Problem Solving (2026)
relevance: Feature: response-time propensity and its interaction with prior proficiency as an effort/learning-rate signal for next-answer correctness.

title: Demszky et al. (2021). Measuring Conversational Uptake: A Case Study on Student-Teacher Interactions
relevance: Feature: automated tutor-uptake score (revoicing/reformulating/answering student ideas) per turn; uptake correlates with instruction quality and achievement.

title: Graesser et al. (2014). Conversations with AutoTutor Help Students Learn (expectation & misconception-tailored dialogue)
relevance: Feature: expectation-coverage and misconception-match scores from semantic comparison of student turns to anticipated correct answers/misconceptions.

title: Macina et al. (2023). MathDial: A Dialogue Tutoring Dataset with Rich Pedagogical Properties Grounded in Math Reasoning
relevance: Dataset + feature: per-turn teacher-move taxonomy (focus/probe/telling, scaffolding questions) to train a move classifier whose distribution feeds the predictor.

title: Scarlatos et al. (2024/2025). Exploring Knowledge Tracing in Tutor-Student Dialogues using LLMs
relevance: Modeling: performs knowledge tracing directly on tutor-student dialogue turns — the closest prior art to predicting correctness from transcripts.

title: Scarlatos et al. (2025). Exploring LLMs for Predicting Tutor Strategy and Student Outcomes in Dialogues
relevance: Modeling: tutor-move sequence features (confirmatory feedback, giving instruction/explanation) predict dialogue-level student success via logistic regression.

title: Enhancing Talk Moves Analysis in Mathematics Tutoring through Classroom Teaching Discourse (2024)
relevance: Feature: accountable-talk move classification (pressing for reasoning, revoicing, encouraging participation) as transcript features from a robust classifier.

title: Demszky & Hill (2022). The NCTE Transcripts: A Dataset of Elementary Math Classroom Transcripts
relevance: Dataset: annotated discourse-move transcripts linked to observation scores/outcomes; source for transfer-learning discourse features into the tutoring model.

title: Shridhar et al. (2022). Automatic Generation of Socratic Subquestions for Teaching Math Word Problems
relevance: Feature: presence and type of scaffolding subquestions vs direct answer-giving as a pedagogy-quality signal tied to guided reasoning.

title: Maurya et al. (2024/2025). Unifying AI Tutor Evaluation: A Taxonomy for Pedagogical Ability Assessment of LLM Tutors
relevance: Modeling: pedagogical rubric dimensions (mistake identification, mistake location, actionability, guidance, coherence) become per-turn quality features.

title: TeachLM: Post-Training LLMs for Education Using Authentic Learning Data (2025)
relevance: Modeling: characterizes behavioral signatures of authentic effective tutoring (adaptivity, questioning) that can be mined as discriminative transcript features.

title: Beyond Prior Knowledge: The Predictive Role of Knowledge-Building in Tutor Learning (2025)
relevance: Feature: ratio of student knowledge-building vs knowledge-telling responses predicts post-test scores over and above prior knowledge.

title: SMART: Simulated Students Aligned with Item Response Theory for Question Difficulty Prediction (2025)
relevance: Feature: an IRT-based predicted difficulty of the upcoming question is a key covariate; near-noise correctness is largely item-difficulty x mastery.

title: Deliberative Discourse Idealized and Realized: Accountable Talk in the Classroom and in Civic Life (Michaels, O'Connor & Resnick, 2008)
relevance: Defines the three accountability dimensions (community, reasoning, knowledge) — encode transcript features counting revoicing, press-for-reasoning, and evidence-grounded moves as tutor-agnostic quality signal.

title: Developing dialogic teaching: genesis, process, trial (Alexander, 2018)
relevance: Alexander's repertoires/indicators give a labeled inventory of dialogic moves that can become a rubric-style feature vector scored per session and correlated with next-item success.

title: Dialogic Teaching: EEF Evaluation Report and Executive Summary (Jay et al., 2017)
relevance: RCT showing ~2 months' attainment gain from dialogic talk — anchors expected effect sizes and validates that discourse-move density is a plausible predictor of learning outcomes.

title: Classroom dialogue: a systematic review across four decades of research (Howe & Abedin, 2013)
relevance: Maps which dialogue organizations are evidence-linked to learning — use as a prior for which move categories to prioritize as features vs. discard as noise.

title: Teacher–student dialogue during classroom teaching: Does it really impact on student outcomes? (Howe, Hennessy, Mercer, Vrikki & Wheatley, 2019)
relevance: Finds elaboration and querying predict attainment only under high-participation ethos — motivates interaction features (move x participation-rate) rather than raw move counts.

title: Classroom Questioning (School Improvement Research Series / Cotton, Education Northwest)
relevance: Synthesis of question cognitive-level and redirection effects — features for share of higher-order vs. recall questions and question-frequency preceding an assessment item.

title: The Role of Wait Time During the Questioning of Children: A Systematic Review (2024, PMC)
relevance: Quantifies wait-time (>3s) effects on response quality — derive timing/pause features between tutor question and student response from timestamped transcripts.

title: What Makes Authentic Questions Authentic? (ERIC EJ1332735)
relevance: Operationalizes Nystrand's authentic-question construct — build a classifier for open/authentic vs. test/known-answer questions as a dialogicity signal feature.

title: Talking and Thinking Together at Key Stage 1 (Mercer, Dawes, et al., Open University ORO)
relevance: Mercer's exploratory/cumulative/disputational talk typology — classify student contributions and use exploratory-talk markers (reasons, 'because', challenge+justify) as features.

title: Classroom Discourse: The Language of Teaching and Learning (Cazden, 1988/2001; ERIC ED288206)
relevance: Foundational account of the IRE/IRF sequence — segment transcripts into IRF triads and use follow-up-move type (evaluate vs. elaborate) as a structural feature.

title: The TalkMoves Dataset: K-12 Mathematics Lesson Transcripts Annotated for Teacher and Student Discursive Moves (Suresh et al., 2022)
relevance: Labeled talk-move corpus to train/transfer a move tagger, then aggregate per-session move distributions as inputs to the correctness predictor.

title: Using Transformers to Provide Teachers with Personalized Feedback on their Classroom Discourse: The TalkMoves Application (Suresh et al., 2021)
relevance: BERT/RoBERTa talk-move classifiers with reported F1 — reusable pretrained move-detection pipeline for extracting features from noisy ASR transcripts.

title: Measuring Five Accountable Talk Moves to Improve Instruction at Scale (2023)
relevance: Scalable detectors for five accountable-talk moves — gives a compact, theory-grounded feature set (keep-together, restate, press-reasoning, etc.) per session.

title: Analyzing Large Language Models for Classroom Discussion Assessment (EDM 2024)
relevance: Compares LLM vs. RoBERTa for discussion-quality scoring — informs whether to use zero-shot LLM discourse scores or fine-tuned taggers as features.

title: The Promises and Pitfalls of Using Language Models to Measure Instruction Quality in Education (2024)
relevance: Documents where LM-derived discourse quality measures fail — guides which automated features are reliable enough to feed a near-noise correctness predictor.

title: Multimodal Assessment of Classroom Discourse Quality: A Text-Centered Attention-Based Multi-Task Learning Approach (2025)
relevance: Shows text+acoustic multi-task modeling of discourse quality — suggests adding prosodic/pause features and multi-task auxiliary discourse-quality heads.

title: Towards Actionable Pedagogical Feedback: A Multi-Perspective Analysis of Mathematics Teaching and Tutoring Dialogue (2025)
relevance: Multi-perspective dialogue analysis bridging teaching and tutoring — a taxonomy of dialogue dimensions to engineer as complementary features.

title: Aligning Tutor Discourse Supporting Rigorous Thinking with Tutee Content Mastery for Predicting Math Achievement (2024)
relevance: Explicitly links tutor rigorous-thinking moves to tutee mastery gains — a template for the exact feature->outcome mapping this project needs, incl. engagement mediation.

title: Interpretable Difficulty-Aware Knowledge Tracing in Tutor-Student Dialogues (Huang et al., 2026)
relevance: IRT-grounded conversational KT mapping LLM outputs to student ability and task difficulty — add difficulty and latent-ability features for next-item correctness prediction.

title: Leveraging LLMs to Assess Tutor Moves in Real-Life Dialogues: A Feasibility Study (2025)
relevance: Tests LLM scoring of tutor moves on authentic (noisy) dialogues — validates feasibility of automated move features on the messy real transcripts this task uses.

title: Discussion Tracker: Supporting Teacher Learning about Students' Collaborative Argumentation in High School Classrooms (Olshefski et al., 2021)
relevance: Classifiers for argument moves, specificity, and collaboration — gives student-side argumentation features (specificity, linking) that may signal comprehension.

title: Argument Component Classification for Classroom Discussions (Lugini & Litman, 2019)
relevance: Detects claims/evidence/warrants in student talk — extract argument-structure counts per student as features correlated with mastery of the assessed content.

title: Annotating Student Talk in Text-based Classroom Discussions (Lugini & Litman, 2019)
relevance: Coding scheme for student-talk dimensions (argumentation, specificity, knowledge domain) — a ready feature schema for characterizing student contributions.

title: Tutor Move Taxonomy: A Theory-Aligned Framework for Analyzing Instructional Moves in Tutoring (Zhou et al., 2026)
relevance: Theory-aligned, AI-annotatable tutor-move taxonomy built to relate tutoring behaviors to learning outcomes — a principled, tutor-agnostic feature ontology for this model.

title: AI-Driven Assessment of Human Tutors: Linking Training Performance to Real-Life Practice (Thomas et al., 2026)
relevance: Generative-AI scoring of tutor practice across 405 sessions — methods for turning transcripts into validated tutor-competency scores usable as session-level features.

title: Productive Discussion Moves in Groups Addressing Controversial Issues (Kim et al., 2026)
relevance: Finds specific interactional patterns (linking emotion to evidence, acknowledging ambiguity) predict discussion quality — candidate higher-order interaction features beyond single-move counts.

title: Deep Knowledge Tracing (Piech et al., 2015)
relevance: Core RNN baseline: encode the transcript as a sequence of (skill, correct) one-hot interactions and predict next-item correctness directly.

title: How Deep is Knowledge Tracing? (Khajah, Lindsey, Mozer, 2016)
relevance: Shows DKT's edge comes from recency, spacing and ability regularities BKT ignores; motivates explicit recency/attempt-count features per KC.

title: Dynamic Key-Value Memory Networks for Knowledge Tracing (Zhang et al., 2017)
relevance: Per-concept memory slots: maintain a running per-KC mastery vector updated across the transcript as a state feature.

title: Addressing Two Problems in DKT via Prediction-Consistent Regularization / DKT+ (Yeung & Yeung, 2018)
relevance: Reconstruction + waviness regularizers give stable per-step mastery; apply a smoothness prior on the predicted correctness trajectory.

title: Performance Factors Analysis - A New Alternative to Knowledge Tracing (Pavlik, Cen, Koedinger, 2009)
relevance: Logistic on per-skill prior success/failure counts; cheapest tutor-agnostic features = running #successes and #failures per KC.

title: Individualized Bayesian Knowledge Tracing Models (Yudelson, Koedinger, Gordon, 2013)
relevance: Per-student learn/guess/slip parameters; add a student-level ability prior estimated from early turns as a feature.

title: A Trainable Spaced Repetition Model for Language Learning / Half-Life Regression (Settles & Meeder, ACL 2016)
relevance: Half-life recall model: time-since-last-practice of the target KC and lag time become explicit forgetting features.

title: A Self-Attentive Model for Knowledge Tracing / SAKT (Pandey & Karypis, 2019)
relevance: Self-attention from the next question to relevant past turns; attention-weighted history handles sparse transcripts.

title: Towards an Appropriate Query, Key, and Value Computation for KT / SAINT (Choi et al., 2020)
relevance: Separated encoder (questions) / decoder (responses) Transformer; lets you stack deep attention over long tutoring transcripts.

title: SAINT+: Integrating Temporal Features for EdNet Correctness Prediction (Shin et al., 2020)
relevance: Adds elapsed-time and lag-time embeddings; response latency and inter-turn gaps are strong tutor-agnostic timing features.

title: Context-Aware Attentive Knowledge Tracing / AKT (Ghosh, Heffernan, Lan, KDD 2020)
relevance: Monotonic exponential-decay attention plus Rasch difficulty embedding; time-decayed context distance and question-difficulty features.

title: Deep-IRT: Making DKT Explainable Using Item Response Theory (Yeung, EDM 2019)
relevance: Emits interpretable ability and item-difficulty per step; use IRT ability x difficulty as theory-grounded features for correctness.

title: Deep Knowledge Tracing is an Implicit Dynamic Multidimensional IRT Model (Converse et al., 2023)
relevance: Theory bridge: justifies interpreting the correctness logit as latent-ability times item-discrimination; guides constrained model heads.

title: Temporal Cross-Effects in Knowledge Tracing / HawkesKT (Wang et al., WSDM 2021)
relevance: Hawkes-process decay of cross-KC influence; build time-decayed cross-concept practice features with per-KC decay rates.

title: Deep Knowledge Tracing with Learning Curves (Chen et al., Frontiers 2023)
relevance: Explicit learning-curve/forgetting term over attempts and elapsed time; per-KC attempt count and time-decay features.

title: A Survey of Knowledge Tracing: Models, Variants, and Applications (Liu et al., 2021)
relevance: Taxonomy (deep / memory / attentive / graph) to enumerate candidate signals and pick a model family for the transcript task.

title: pyKT: A Python Library to Benchmark Deep Learning based KT Models (Liu et al., NeurIPS 2022)
relevance: Warns of label leakage in one-by-one evaluation; adopt its all-in-one protocol and reference implementations to avoid inflated correctness AUC.

title: simpleKT: A Simple But Tough-to-Beat Baseline for Knowledge Tracing (Liu et al., ICLR 2023)
relevance: Rasch question-difficulty embedding + plain dot-product attention; a strong minimal baseline and a scalar question-difficulty feature.

title: Enhancing Deep Knowledge Tracing with Auxiliary Tasks / AT-DKT (Liu et al., WWW 2023)
relevance: Auxiliary question-tagging and history-prediction tasks improve next-response accuracy; multitask objective for weak transcript signal.

title: Towards Robust Knowledge Tracing Models via k-Sparse Attention / sparseKT (Huang et al., SIGIR 2023)
relevance: Keep only top-k relevant past turns; robustness recipe for noisy or long tutoring histories.

title: DKT2: Revisiting Applicable and Comprehensive Knowledge Tracing in Large-Scale Data (2025)
relevance: Recent xLSTM + IRT practical recipe balancing accuracy and deployability; modern reference architecture for correctness prediction.

title: Next Token Knowledge Tracing: Exploiting Pretrained LLM Representations to Decode Student Behaviour (2025)
relevance: Reframes KT as next-token prediction using LLM embeddings of question text; supplies tutor-agnostic semantic features of each item.

title: A Systematic Review of Knowledge Tracing and Large Language Models (2024)
relevance: Maps LLM-KT design choices (prompting, embeddings, fusion) for text transcripts; guides how to combine LLM signal with sequence models.

title: Language Bottleneck Models for Qualitative Knowledge State Modeling (2025)
relevance: Compresses a transcript into a natural-language knowledge-state summary used to predict correctness - an interpretable text-feature bottleneck.

title: EdNet: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Dataset in Education (Choi et al., 2019)
relevance: Large interaction dataset with rich timing/action fields for pretraining and for defining tutor-agnostic behavioral features.

title: GIKT: A Graph-based Interaction Model for Knowledge Tracing (Yang et al., 2020)
relevance: Question-KC bipartite graph aggregation; use skill-relatedness between the next question and past turns as a similarity feature.

title: HiTSKT: A Hierarchical Transformer Model for Session-Aware Knowledge Tracing (Ke et al., 2022)
relevance: Two-level within-session / across-session attention; models the session structure of a tutoring transcript and cross-session forgetting.

title: Corbett & Anderson (1995), Knowledge Tracing: Modeling the Acquisition of Procedural Knowledge
relevance: Foundational BKT: per-skill latent mastery probability with learn/guess/slip params — yields a running P(mastery) feature per knowledge component to feed next-question correctness.

title: Scarlatos et al. (2025), Training LLM-based Tutors to Improve Student Learning Outcomes in Dialogues
relevance: Uses an LLM student model that scores P(correct next response) given dialogue history — essentially the exact modeling target we want to predict.

title: Boyer et al. (2008), Learner Characteristics and Feedback in Tutorial Dialogue
relevance: Prior knowledge + dialogue-act and feedback features predict outcomes — early evidence for combining learner-state with discourse features.

title: Suresh et al. (2021), What Would a Teacher Do? Predicting Future Talk Moves
relevance: Sequence modeling of accountable-talk moves — talk-move transition features and next-move prediction as context for student response quality.

title: Maurya et al. (2025), Findings of the BEA 2025 Shared Task on Pedagogical Ability Assessment of AI-powered Tutors
relevance: Defines mistake-identification/location/guidance/actionability dimensions on tutor turns — pedagogical-quality features of the preceding tutor move.

title: Vail et al. (2024), Automatically Detecting Confusion and Conflict During Collaborative Learning Using Linguistic, Prosodic, and Facial Cues
relevance: Text/linguistic cues that detect confusion — concrete NLP features (hedges, disfluency, negations) computable directly from a transcript.

title: Forbes-Riley & Litman (2008), Responding to Student Uncertainty During Computer Tutoring: An Experimental Evaluation
relevance: Student uncertainty (hedging, pauses) is a decisive tutoring signal — uncertainty markers per student turn as a predictor of next-answer errors.

title: Recognizing Uncertainty in Speech (2011)
relevance: Lexical/prosodic uncertainty-cue inventory — a ready lexicon of hedging/uncertainty words to featurize student turns in text transcripts.

title: Early Detection of Wheel-Spinning: Comparison Across Tutors (Gong & Beck)
relevance: Wheel-spinning = many attempts without mastery — an unproductive-persistence feature (attempts-without-3-in-a-row) predicting continued failure.

title: Rastrollo-Guerrero et al. (2021), Educational Data Mining Techniques for Student Performance Prediction: Method Review
relevance: Open-access survey of features/algorithms for performance prediction — grounds baseline feature sets and model choices (RF, RNN) for the task.

title: Predicting Student's Performance Through Data Mining (2021)
relevance: End-to-end EDM feature engineering + classifier pipeline — a concrete template for turning heterogeneous session signals into a correctness classifier.

title: Knowledge Distillation in RNN-Attention Models for Early Prediction of Student Performance (2024)
relevance: Attention over partial sequences for early prediction — architecture for predicting outcomes from short/truncated transcripts (near-noise, few turns).

title: Identifying At-Risk K-12 Students in Multimodal Online Environments (2020)
relevance: Multimodal at-risk classifier — feature-fusion recipe for combining engagement, behavior, and content signals when any single signal is weak.

title: Huang et al. (2017), Question Difficulty Prediction for Reading Problems in Standard Tests
relevance: Predicts item difficulty from question text via neural models — a text-derived difficulty feature for the upcoming assessment question.

title: Benedetto et al. (2020), Introducing a Framework to Assess Newly Created Questions with NLP
relevance: NLP features to calibrate question difficulty/discrimination without response data — cold-start difficulty features for the next item's text.

title: Zhang et al. (2022), Automatic Short Math Answer Grading via In-context Meta-learning (EDM 2022)
relevance: Scores semantic correctness of open student responses with few examples — turn a free-text student answer in the transcript into a correct/incorrect feature.

title: The Power of Feedback (Hattie & Timperley, 2007)
relevance: Feature: tag each tutor turn by feedback level (task / process / self-regulation / self) and by which of the three questions it answers (feed-up/back/forward) — level composition of a session as a predictor.

title: Focus on Formative Feedback (Shute, 2008)
relevance: Feature: code feedback type (verification vs. elaboration/hint/worked-example), timing, and specificity per turn; her interaction rules give conditions where richer feedback should help vs. hurt.

title: The Effects of Feedback Interventions on Performance: FIT (Kluger & DeNisi, 1996)
relevance: Modeling idea: a ~1/3 of feedback lowers performance — add a 'feedback-directs-attention-to-self' signal (praise/person-focused turns) as a negative predictor of next-item correctness.

title: Inside the Black Box: Raising Standards Through Classroom Assessment (Black & Wiliam, 1998)
relevance: Session-level feature: detect a complete formative loop (elicit evidence → interpret → act/adjust) in the transcript; presence/closure of the loop as tutor-agnostic quality signal.

title: Feedback and Self-Regulated Learning: A Theoretical Synthesis (Butler & Winne, 1995)
relevance: Feature: markers of student self-monitoring / internal feedback (self-checks, revised answers) — internally-generated feedback predicts uptake independent of tutor.

title: The Critical Role of Retrieval Practice / Test-Enhanced Learning (Roediger & Butler, 2011)
relevance: Feature: count retrieval attempts vs. re-study before the target item, and whether correct-answer feedback followed errors — right/wrong-only feedback is weak; corrected retrieval predicts gains.

title: Desirable Difficulties in Theory and Practice (Bjork & Bjork, 2020)
relevance: Feature: spacing, interleaving, and generation/struggle indicators in the session — 'harder-now' conditions depress current accuracy but should raise next-item transfer, a key confound to model.

title: Eliciting Self-Explanations Improves Understanding (Chi et al.)
relevance: Feature: quantify student self-explanation / reasoning-rich turns (why/because, principle references) vs. shallow acknowledgments as a positive next-correct signal.

title: A Meta-Analysis of Feedback Timing on Learning in Computer-Assisted Learning (2026)
relevance: Feature: encode feedback delay as intervening-items or elapsed-time, plus feedback focus/assessment type moderators — timing effect is null on average but strongly moderated, so interact these features.

title: The Effect of Formative Assessment Practices on Student Learning: A Meta-Analysis
relevance: Feature: distinguish student-initiated vs. adult-initiated vs. computer-initiated feedback (effect sizes 1.16 / 0.69 / 0.42) — who initiates the formative move is a strong effect moderator to encode.

title: A Systematic Review of Meta-Analyses on Formative Assessment in K-12 (Sustainability, 2024)
relevance: Map of which formative-assessment moderators (feedback quality, engagement, self-regulation) carry effect — a checklist of theory-grounded features and priors for a near-noise task.

title: Effectiveness of Formative Assessment for Reading Achievement in K-12: A Meta-Analysis (Frontiers, 2022)
relevance: Moderator evidence (feedback frequency, agent, subject) for setting realistic effect-size priors and choosing which transcript features are worth extracting.

title: Properties of the Bayesian Knowledge Tracing Model (Van de Sande)
relevance: Modeling idea: BKT's learn/slip/guess/prior parameters as an interpretable latent-mastery baseline; slip/guess directly model the near-noise floor on next-item correctness.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Knowledge Tracing Models
relevance: Modeling idea: calibrated predictive uncertainty for a near-noise target — abstain/confidence outputs so the model reports when signal is genuinely absent.

title: Towards Pedagogically Aligned LLM Tutors for Math Mistake Remediation
relevance: Feature: scaffolding, factuality, and answer-leakage indicators of a tutor turn — leaked answers vs. genuine scaffolding change what 'next correct' actually measures.

title: The Impact of Off-Task and Gaming Behaviors on Learning (Cocea, Hershkovitz & Baker)
relevance: Feature: detect gaming/off-task patterns (rapid guessing, hint-abuse, systematic wrong-then-right) — behavioral negatives that predict failure regardless of tutor content.

title: Affect-Driven Learning Outcomes Prediction in Intelligent Tutoring Systems (Joshi et al.)
relevance: Modeling idea: fuse affect + disengagement + performance to predict outcomes — engaged concentration (and even frustration) as features alongside raw correctness history.

title: 'Good job!' The Impact of Positive and Negative Feedback on Performance (2023)
relevance: Feature: sentiment/valence and directiveness of tutor feedback turns — positive vs. corrective/negative feedback have asymmetric effects on subsequent performance.

title: LLMs Approach Expert Pedagogical Quality in Math Tutoring but Differ in Profiles (2025)
relevance: Feature: instructional and linguistic profile features (questioning rate, explanation density, lexical hedging) that separate effective from ineffective tutoring turns.

title: The ICAP Framework: Linking Cognitive Engagement to Active Learning Outcomes (Chi & Wylie, 2014)
relevance: Core theory: label each student turn Passive/Active/Constructive/Interactive; per-session ICAP-mode distribution and highest-mode-reached become the primary engagement features.

title: Applying the ICAP Framework to Improve Classroom Learning (Chi & Boucher)
relevance: Gives concrete overt-behavior exemplars per mode (note-taking vs. explaining vs. co-constructing) — a coding rubric you can turn into an utterance classifier for automatic ICAP tagging.

title: Putting ICAP to the Test: technology-enhanced learning activities vs. cognitive & affective-motivational outcomes (Scientific Reports, 2024)
relevance: Cautionary recent evidence that the strict I>C>A>P ordering doesn't always hold — motivates modeling individual activity types/features rather than a single ordinal engagement score.

title: Active Learning Through Discussion: ICAP Framework for Education in Health Professions (BMC Med Educ, 2020)
relevance: Shows self-study + question-generation before discussion predicts post-test score, mediated by mental effort — supports a 'did the student generate questions?' feature and effort proxies.

title: Evaluation of Studies Based on the ICAP Framework in Learning Environments
relevance: Survey of how ICAP has been operationalized across studies — a menu of measurable behavioral proxies for each mode to engineer as features.

title: School Engagement: Potential of the Concept / Academic Engagement Overview (ERIC EJ1121524)
relevance: Fredricks-style behavioral/emotional/cognitive engagement taxonomy — separate transcript feature groups (participation counts, sentiment/affect, depth-of-processing markers).

title: Question Asking During Tutoring (Graesser & Person, 1994)
relevance: Deep-reasoning question QUALITY (not frequency) correlates with achievement — feature: count of student deep/why-how questions via the GPH question taxonomy.

title: AutoTutor: An Intelligent Tutoring System with Mixed-Initiative Dialogue
relevance: Expectation-Misconception-Tailored dialogue: features = fraction of 'expectations' the student verbalized and misconception markers present before the assessment item.

title: Modeling One-on-one Online Tutoring Discourse using an Accountable Talk Framework (EDM 2022)
relevance: Adapts accountable-talk (accountability to community/knowledge/rigorous thinking) to 1:1 tutoring — a tutor-agnostic move taxonomy for feature engineering.

title: Brief but Impactful: How Human Tutoring Interactions Shape Engagement in Online Learning (2026)
relevance: Aligns transcript conversational moves with moment-to-moment productivity gains — method for pinpointing which tutor/student moves precede success on the next item.

title: From Words to Wisdom: Discourse Annotation and Baseline Models for Student Dialogue Understanding (2025)
relevance: Student-side discourse annotation schema plus baseline classifiers — schema and models for turning raw student turns into engagement/understanding features.

title: Combining Log Data and Collaborative Dialogue Features to Predict Project Quality in Middle School AI Education (2025)
relevance: Shows Sentence-BERT dialogue embeddings + structured dialogue features predict an outcome — an embedding+handcrafted feature-fusion recipe for near-noise prediction.

title: Predicting Cognitive Engagement in Online Course Discussion Forums (Gorgun & Bulut, EDM 2022; ERIC ED624102)
relevance: Hand-codes posts to ICAP-style cognitive-engagement levels then trains ML on linguistic/contextual features — a concrete feature list for automatic engagement scoring of student text.

title: Knowledge Co-construction and Initiative in Peer Learning Interactions (AIED 2009)
relevance: Initiative shifts and knowledge co-construction episodes correlate with learning — feature: who-drives-the-turn ratio and count of co-construction exchanges.

title: Exploratory Talk / Critical Thinking via WebQuest (Mercer tradition; PMC7471859)
relevance: Classify talk as disputational/cumulative/exploratory; reasoning-word markers ('because','if','I think','why') distinguish exploratory (reasoning-visible) talk that predicts gains.

title: Learning Engagement in Massive Open Online Courses (Frontiers in Education, 2022)
relevance: Operationalizes cognitive/emotional/behavioral engagement from text and links to achievement — validated indicator set for scoring student contributions in transcripts.

title: Interactive-engagement vs. Traditional Methods (Hake, 1998)
relevance: Foundational 'interactive engagement' evidence with normalized-gain metric; motivates using an interactivity index of the session as a predictor and normalized-gain as target framing.

title: Chi et al. (1994) / SERT lineage — Self-Explanation and Reading Strategy Training (SERT) manual
relevance: Defines the five self-explanation strategies (monitoring, paraphrase, elaboration, prediction, bridging) → codeable transcript labels; strategy mix per student is a signal.

title: Flavell (1979) — Metacognition and Cognitive Monitoring: A New Area of Cognitive-Developmental Inquiry
relevance: Origin of the metacognition construct (knowledge/experience/goals/strategies) → framework for tagging metacognitive utterances (monitoring statements) as features.

title: The Future of Metacognition Research: Balancing Construct Breadth with Measurement Rigor (2024)
relevance: Modern taxonomy of metacognition measures → guides which monitoring/control signals are reliably extractable from text vs noise; feature-validity checklist.

title: Student Metacognitive Monitoring: Predicting Test Achievement (calibration/JOL study)
relevance: Judgments-of-learning calibration predicts test performance → feature: student confidence/hedging language and calibration gap vs actual correctness.

title: A Review of Self-Regulated Learning: Six Models and Four Directions for Research (Panadero, 2017)
relevance: Synthesizes Zimmerman/Winne-Hadwin/Pintrich SRL phases → structured schema of forethought/monitoring/reflection acts to annotate transcripts and engineer phase-coverage features.

title: Greene & Azevedo (2007) — A Theoretical Review of Winne & Hadwin's COPES Model of SRL
relevance: COPES (conditions/operations/products/evaluations/standards) → maps each dialogue move to a regulatory operation; monitoring-density feature per problem.

title: Aleven & Koedinger — Investigations into Help Seeking and Learning with a Cognitive Tutor
relevance: Links help-request patterns and prior knowledge to learning outcomes → features: hint-request rate, timing relative to errors, help-then-retry success.

title: Roll, Aleven, McLaren & Koedinger — Improving Students' Help-Seeking Skills Using Metacognitive Feedback
relevance: Operationalizes a (meta)cognitive help-seeking model with rules for good/bad help acts → rule-based features flagging help abuse/avoidance in a transcript.

title: Baker, Corbett, Roll & Koedinger — Detection and Analysis of Off-Task Gaming Behavior in ITS
relevance: Machine-learned gaming detector using speed/error/hint patterns → transferable behavioral features (rapid guessing, hint drilling) that suppress correctness.

title: Baker & Rossi (2013) — Assessing the Disengaged Behaviors of Learners
relevance: Catalog of disengagement (gaming, off-task, carelessness) and their detectors → engagement-state feature set; carelessness explains wrong answers despite mastery.

title: Roediger & Karpicke (2006) — The Power of Testing Memory: Basic Research and Implications for Practice
relevance: Retrieval practice > restudy for retention → feature: whether the target skill was actively retrieved earlier in the session (not just shown/told) predicts next-question correctness.

title: Karpicke & Roediger (2007) — Repeated Retrieval During Learning Is the Key to Long-Term Retention
relevance: Number/spacing of successful retrievals drives retention → feature: count of prior correct retrievals and spacing of the skill within the transcript.

title: Kapur — Productive Failure in Learning the Concept of Variance
relevance: Pre-instruction struggle/impasse can boost later success → feature: presence of a productive-impasse episode (generate-explore then consolidate) preceding the assessment item.

title: Kapur & Roll — Productive Failure (overview chapter)
relevance: Four core mechanisms of productive failure → non-monotonic feature: early errors + reflection may positively, not negatively, predict the next answer.

title: A Comprehensive Survey on Deep Learning Techniques in Educational Data Mining (2023)
relevance: Maps DKT/AKT/transformer KT architectures and inputs → menu of sequence-model backbones and interaction encodings for correctness prediction.

title: A Survey of Explainable Knowledge Tracing (2024)
relevance: Interpretable KT feature families (skill mastery estimates, attention over past items) → interpretable, tutor-agnostic features to add alongside a black-box model.

title: MetaCLASS: Metacognitive Coaching for Learning with Adaptive Self-Regulation Support (2026)
relevance: Defines 11 interpretable metacognitive coaching moves (incl. 'stay silent') tied to SRL → move-taxonomy features; also flags that intervention density is itself a signal.

title: Self-Regulated Learning Processes in Secondary Education: A Network Analysis of Trace-Based Measures (2024)
relevance: Builds SRL-process networks from fine-grained traces → feature: transition patterns between regulatory acts (e.g., monitor→revise) rather than isolated act counts.

title: Process Mining for Self-Regulated Learning Assessment in e-Learning (2024)
relevance: Inductive-Miner SRL process models differentiate pass vs fail students → feature: conformance of a session's act-sequence to a 'successful-learner' process model.

title: Using Think-Aloud Data to Understand Relations Between Self-Regulation Cycle Characteristics and Student Performance in ITS (2023)
relevance: Relates completeness/quality of SRL cycles to performance → feature: whether a full plan-monitor-evaluate cycle occurred before the assessment item.

title: Understanding Gaming the System by Analyzing Self-Regulated Learning in Think-Aloud Protocols (LAK 2026)
relevance: Recasts gaming as maladaptive SRL (long utterances, error recognition, low planning) → verbal features distinguishing shallow gaming from genuine reasoning in transcripts.

title: Analyzing Adaptive Scaffolds That Help Students Develop Self-Regulated Learning Behaviors (2022)
relevance: Shows which scaffold-response patterns build SRL → feature: student uptake of scaffolds (acts on hint/prompt vs ignores) as a mediator of next-item success.

title: MiBoard: iSTART Metacognitive Training Through Gaming (self-explanation auto-scoring lineage)
relevance: Describes automated evaluation of self-explanation quality (word-match/LSA/topic models) → pipeline for scoring a student's explanation turn into a numeric quality feature.

title: Exploring the Effectiveness of Using LLMs for Automated Assessment of Student Self-Explanations in Programming Education (2026)
relevance: Benchmarks LLM vs semantic-similarity scoring of student self-explanations → concrete method to convert free-text self-explanations into a correctness-predictive quality score.

title: Identifying and Mitigating Answer Bias in Knowledge Tracing
relevance: Warns that KT models exploit answer-label bias; motivates debiasing/regularization crucial for a near-noise correctness-prediction task.

title: Language Model Can Do Knowledge Tracing (LKT)
relevance: Injects pretrained LM embeddings of question/concept text into KT, letting the semantic content of the next problem inform the prediction.

title: Automated Knowledge Concept Annotation and Question Representation Learning for Knowledge Tracing
relevance: LLM auto-labels the knowledge component of each turn/question, giving per-turn KC tags without a hand-authored Q-matrix.

title: Academic Literacy in Mathematics for English Learners (Moschkovich, 2015)
relevance: Theory of the mathematical register: use of specialized vocabulary, justification and explanation are language markers of understanding to extract from student turns.

title: Understanding Student Language: An Unsupervised Dialogue Act Classification
relevance: Unsupervised clustering of student utterances into dialogue acts; the distribution of student act types (question/short-answer/substantive) is a feature.

title: Diagnosing Bugs in a Simple Procedural Skill (Burton; Brown & Burton buggy models)
relevance: Systematic 'bugs' as discrete perturbations of a correct procedure: match student errors to a bug library to produce error-type indicator features.

title: A Revaluation of Newman's Error Analysis (White, 2009)
relevance: Five-stage error taxonomy (reading/comprehension/transformation/process/encoding) lets you feature which stage a student's error falls in.

title: Results and Insights from Diagnostic Questions: The NeurIPS 2020 Education Challenge (Eedi)
relevance: 20M MCQ answers whose distractors encode misconceptions; which distractor is chosen is a misconception signal plus large-scale correctness-prediction baselines.

title: Do LLMs Make Mistakes Like Students? Exploring Natural Alignment between Language Models and Human Error Patterns
relevance: LLM answer-option perplexity aligns with human error patterns, so LLM likelihood over answer choices is a difficulty/misconception proxy feature.

title: Cognitive-Uncertainty Guided Knowledge Distillation for Accurate Classification of Student Misconceptions
relevance: Classifies the specific misconception behind a student response with uncertainty-aware distillation; predicted misconception label + confidence become features.

title: Decision Tree Modeling of Wheel-Spinning and Productive Persistence in Skill Builders
relevance: Detects unproductive persistence: attempt counts, time-on-skill and repeated-error streaks predict continued failure, i.e. a likely incorrect next answer.

title: MathTutorBench: A Benchmark for Measuring Open-ended Pedagogical Capabilities of LLM Tutors
relevance: Benchmark of tutor mistake-diagnosis and scaffolding skills, supplying pedagogically-labeled dialogues to derive tutor-move features and evaluation dimensions.

title: Simulating Students with Large Language Models: A Review of Architecture, Mechanisms, and Role Modelling
relevance: How to generate synthetic student trajectories/errors for data augmentation and cold-start of a correctness predictor, with caveats on validity.

title: A Selective Review of Affect-Sensitive Instructional Strategies (D'Mello, Blanchard, Baker, Ocumpaugh, Brawner)
relevance: Catalogs which affect states help vs hurt learning and valid responses; supplies label priors/sign for a tutor-agnostic affect→next-correct model.

title: More Confusion and Frustration, Better Learning: The Impact of Erroneous Examples (Richey et al., 2019)
relevance: Induced confusion/frustration raised learning; feature = count and resolution of error-driven confusion episodes as a positive predictor of next-item success.

title: Emotional Learning Analytics (HLA Handbook Ch.12, D'Mello & Jensen)
relevance: Survey of sensor-free affect detection from log/text; blueprint for building transcript-only, tutor-agnostic affect features.

title: Affective States and State Tests: How Affect/Engagement Predict End-of-Year Learning (Pardos, Baker, San Pedro, Gowda, Gowda)
relevance: Session-level fractions of boredom/engagement/confusion/frustration predict test scores; feature = affect proportions, with context-dependent sign (boredom hurts in problem-solving, not in scaffolding).

title: From Reaction to Anticipation: Predicting Future Affect (Zambrano, Baker, Baral, Heffernan, Lan, EDM 2024)
relevance: Confusion/frustration/boredom predictable tens of seconds ahead from logs; feature = short-horizon affect forecasts as leading indicators of the next error.

title: Carelessness and Affect in an Intelligent Tutoring System for Mathematics (San Pedro, Baker et al.)
relevance: Contextual-slip detector separates 'knows-but-slipped' from 'doesn't-know'; feature = per-response slip probability to de-noise next-correct labels.

title: Carelessness Detection using Performance Factor Analysis (Zhang, Baker, Srivastava, Ocumpaugh, Mills, McLaren, 2025)
relevance: New carelessness operationalization; feature = response-time × prior-success interaction to flag careless errors that otherwise mislead a correctness model.

title: Detecting/Developing a Generalizable Detector of When Students Game the System (Baker, Corbett, Roll, Koedinger, 2008)
relevance: Latency + rapid-guess + hint-abuse features for gaming; behavioral signals that both cause and predict non-mastery on the next item.

title: BROMP Quantitative Field Observations: A Review (Ocumpaugh, Baker, Rodrigo)
relevance: Standardized affect/behavior label scheme and observable cue lists; defines target categories and ground-truth protocol for training detectors.

title: DAiSEE: Dataset for Affective States in E-Environments (boredom/confusion/engagement/frustration, in the wild)
relevance: Labeled in-the-wild affect video; transfer-learning source and a concrete four-state affect ontology to reuse for transcript-aligned labels.

title: EmotiW 2018: Audio-Video, Student Engagement and Group-Level Affect Prediction (Dhall et al.)
relevance: Engagement-in-the-wild benchmark; baseline temporal-video engagement features and evaluation setup for ordinal engagement.

title: Improving SOTA in Detecting Student Engagement with ResNet and TCN Hybrid Network
relevance: Temporal CNN over frame features; a sequence-modeling template for aggregating per-moment engagement across a whole session.

title: Engagement Measurement Based on Facial Landmarks and Spatial-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks
relevance: Landmark-graph features give a low-dimensional, identity-robust engagement signal—useful if any behavioral/timing analog is available per turn.

title: A General Model for Detecting Learner Engagement: Implementation and Evaluation
relevance: Cross-dataset generalizable engagement model; directly addresses the tutor-agnostic transfer problem central to our task.

title: Inconsistencies in the Definition and Annotation of Student Engagement in Virtual Learning Datasets: A Critical Review
relevance: Documents label noise/construct-validity issues in engagement labels; informs robust-label training and metric choice for our near-noise prediction problem.

title: Early Detection of Wheel Spinning: Comparison across Tutors, Models, Features, Operationalizations (Zhang et al., EDM 2019)
relevance: Predicts eventual non-mastery from the first few opportunities across multiple tutors; feature = early struggle-streak signals that generalize tutor-to-tutor.

title: Developing Early Detectors of Student Attrition and Wheel Spinning Using Deep Learning (Botelho, Varatharaj, Patikorn, Doherty, Adjei, Beck, 2019)
relevance: RNN over the raw action sequence for wheel-spinning/attrition; end-to-end sequence representation feeding next-outcome prediction.

title: The Role of Mastery Learning in Intelligent Tutoring Systems: Principal Stratification on a Latent Variable
relevance: Causal framing of mastery vs mere practice; guards against confounds when correlating in-session behavior with next-correct outcomes.

title: Extending Deep Knowledge Tracing: Inferring Interpretable Knowledge and Predicting Post-System Performance
relevance: DKT variant that predicts downstream/held-out performance rather than in-loop—closer to our 'next assessment question' target.

title: Ensembling LLMs to Characterize Affective Dynamics in Student-AI Tutor Dialogues (Zhang, Alghowinem, Breazeal, ACII 2025)
relevance: Zero-shot LLM valence/arousal/learning-helpfulness + emotion labels per turn, rank-weighted fused—a ready-made transcript affect feature extractor for our model.

title: Tutor CoPilot: A Human-AI Approach for Scaling Real-Time Expertise
relevance: Identifies effective real-time tutoring moves linked to gains; move-type and timing features to extract from dialogue as outcome predictors.

title: Dropout Prediction over Weeks in MOOCs by Learning Representations of Clicks and Videos
relevance: Learns sequence representations of interaction to predict disengagement/attrition—representation-learning template for encoding a whole session transcript.

title: Enhancing Online Learning by Integrating Biosensors and Multimodal Learning Analytics for Detecting and Predicting Student Behavior: A Review
relevance: Inventory of modalities, features, and fusion strategies for detecting and predicting behavior—menu of candidate signals to approximate from text-only logs.

title: Measuring the Impact of Student Gaming Behaviors on Learner Modeling
relevance: Quantifies how gaming corrupts knowledge estimates; motivates gaming-aware masking/features to reduce label noise when predicting next-question correctness.

title: Wood, Bruner & Ross (1976), The Role of Tutoring in Problem Solving
relevance: Scaffolding/ZPD theory yields features counting the six scaffolding functions (task simplification, marking critical features, frustration control) and whether support is faded before the next question.

title: Stasaski et al. (2020), CIMA: A Large Open Access Dialogue Dataset for Tutoring
relevance: Tutoring dialogues with labeled tutor response types and grounded concepts; supply gold tutor-move labels to train move-classifiers used as transcript features.

title: Caines et al. (2020/2022), The Teacher-Student Chatroom Corpus (TSCC)
relevance: Authentic ESL teacher-student chat with proficiency assessment; tutor-agnostic dialogue-act and repair patterns transferable as features across subjects.

title: Enhancing Talk Moves Analysis in Mathematics Tutoring through Classroom Teaching Discourse (COLING 2025)
relevance: Accountable-Talk 'talk move' classifiers (student and tutor) give per-turn categorical features (making a claim, providing evidence, revoicing) predictive of understanding.

title: Kwon et al. (2024), BIPED: Pedagogically Informed Tutoring System for ESL Education
relevance: Defines 34 tutor acts and 9 student acts with a labeled corpus; a rich dialogue-act taxonomy to featurize each turn of a transcript.

title: LearnLM Team, Google (2024), LearnLM: Improving Gemini for Learning
relevance: 'Pedagogical instruction following' attributes (encourage active learning, manage load, stimulate curiosity, adapt) give a checklist of tutor-behavior features to detect in transcripts.

title: Wang et al. (2024), Bridging the Novice-Expert Gap via Models of Decision-Making: Remediating Math Mistakes (Bridge)
relevance: Decompose each remediation into (error, strategy, intention); these decision-model labels become structured features characterizing how a mistake was addressed before the next item.

title: Yan et al. (2023), Practical and Ethical Challenges of LLMs in Education: A Systematic Scoping Review
relevance: Landscape and caveats (bias, reliability, over-reliance) to scope realistic, generalizable signal and avoid tutor-specific leakage in a near-noise prediction task.

title: qDKT: Question-centric Deep Knowledge Tracing (Sonkar et al., 2020)
relevance: Track at question granularity (not just skill) with Laplacian smoothing across similar items, handling sparse per-question history in a transcript.

title: Modeling Individualization in a Bayesian Networks Implementation of Knowledge Tracing (Pardos & Heffernan, 2010)
relevance: Prior-per-student (P-init) node to seed a first-question-correct-probability feature before any in-session evidence accrues.

title: Instructional Factors Analysis (Chi, Koedinger, Gordon, et al.)
relevance: Logistic model separating effects of prior successes, failures, and instructional/tell events (like tutor hints) on next-attempt success — direct transcript-event features.

title: Using an Additive Factor Model and Performance Factor Analysis to Assess Learning Gains (Shi & Pavlik, EDM 2017)
relevance: AFM/PFA logistic features: per-skill opportunity count and cumulative success/failure counts as cheap, theory-grounded correctness predictors.

title: R2DE: A NLP Approach to Estimating IRT Parameters of Newly Generated Questions (Benedetto et al., LAK 2020)
relevance: Regress IRT difficulty/discrimination from question + option text to solve the cold-start when the next item has no response history.

title: A Quantitative Study of NLP Approaches to Question Difficulty Estimation (Benedetto, 2023)
relevance: Benchmarks Transformer vs TF-IDF/word2vec/linguistic features across domains — guidance on which text-difficulty encoder to bolt onto the next question.

title: Item Difficulty Prediction Using Item Text Features: Comparison Across ML Algorithms (Štěpánek et al., Mathematics 2023, open access)
relevance: Concrete engineered text-feature set (length, syllables, POS, embeddings) with elastic-net winning — a compact difficulty-feature block.

title: Estimating Item Difficulty Using LLMs and Tree-Based ML Algorithms (2025)
relevance: Have an LLM extract cognitive/linguistic difficulty features from item text, then feed random-forest/GBM — a response-free difficulty prior for the next question.

title: The Impact of Item-Writing Flaws on Difficulty and Discrimination in IRT (2025)
relevance: Item-writing-flaw rubric scores (ambiguity, negation, implausible distractors) as pre-deployment textual features correlated with empirical difficulty.

title: NLP Methods May Actually Be Better Than Professors at Estimating Question Difficulty (2025)
relevance: Evidence that automated text-difficulty estimates beat expert judgment — supports trusting a model-derived difficulty feature over heuristics.

title: Are You Doubtful? Model Uncertainty for Question Difficulty Estimation (2024)
relevance: Use an LLM's answer-probability/entropy on the item as a proxy difficulty signal that predicts whether a student will get it right.

title: Beyond Flesch-Kincaid: Prompt-based Metrics Improve Difficulty Classification of Educational Texts (2024)
relevance: Replace shallow readability indices with prompt-based semantic-difficulty scores as a stronger linguistic-complexity feature for the question stem.

title: Psychometric Alignment: Capturing Human Knowledge Distributions via Language Models (2024)
relevance: Measures how well an LM reproduces the human difficulty/ability distribution — a validation lens and a way to calibrate simulated-student difficulty features.

title: Lost in Benchmarks? Rethinking LLM Benchmarking with Item Response Theory (2025)
relevance: Large-scale IRT fitting recipe (ability, difficulty, discrimination) over item-response matrices, transferable to fitting per-student/per-item params from transcripts.

title: Large Language Model Psychometrics: A Systematic Review (2025)
relevance: Survey of psychometric constructs (latent ability, reliability, validity) and how to operationalize them as model features/evaluation for ability estimation.

title: Deep Computerized Adaptive Testing (2025)
relevance: Fisher-information item selection and ability-tracking from sparse responses — informs how to weight the most-informative prior turns when estimating current ability.

title: Early Detection of Wheel-Spinning in ASSISTments (Botelho, Baker, et al.)
relevance: Unproductive-persistence detector from first few attempts (consecutive failures) — a struggling-student feature predicting continued incorrectness.

title: Pushing on Text Readability Assessment: A Transformer Meets Handcrafted Linguistic Features (Lee, Jang & Lee, 2021)
relevance: Fuse Transformer embeddings with handcrafted linguistic-complexity features — a hybrid encoder for scoring the difficulty of the next question's text.

title: M-Powering Teachers: NLP-Powered Feedback Improves 1:1 Instruction and Student Outcomes (Demszky et al., L@S 2023)
relevance: Shows three cheap, validated dialogic metrics (uptake of student ideas, tutor talk-time share, question rate) causally move outcomes; use all three as session-level features.

title: Dialogue Act Modeling for Automatic Tagging and Recognition of Conversational Speech (Stolcke, Jurafsky et al., Comp. Linguistics 2000)
relevance: Foundational HMM over dialogue-act sequences with lexical/prosodic cues; motivates modeling the transcript as a DA n-gram/sequence and using DA-transition features (e.g., question->answer->uptake).

title: Does Informativeness Matter? Active Learning for Educational Dialogue Act Classification (Lin et al., 2023)
relevance: Educational DA classifier plus sample-informativeness; yields a labeled DA feature stream and a way to prioritize which utterances carry the most signal about student state.

title: Robust Educational Dialogue Act Classifiers with Low-Resource and Imbalanced Datasets (Lin et al., AIED 2023)
relevance: Recipes for training DA classifiers on small, skewed tutoring data; directly usable to build the per-utterance tutor/student act-tagging feature extractor for our pipeline.

title: Automated Classification of Tutors' Dialogue Acts Using Generative AI: A Case Study on the CIMA Corpus (2025)
relevance: Zero/few-shot LLM tagging of tutor moves (question/hint/correction/confirmation); gives a no-train feature extractor for tutor-move distributions preceding each assessment item.

title: IntrEx: A Dataset for Modeling Engagement in Educational Conversations (Findings EMNLP 2025)
relevance: Links linguistic/cognitive drivers (concreteness, readability, uptake) to turn-level interestingness; supplies computable engagement features hypothesized to precede better performance.

title: Why Do Only Some Events Cause Learning During Human Tutoring? (VanLehn et al., Cognition & Instruction 2003)
relevance: Impasse hypothesis: learning follows student impasses/errors, not tutor explanations per se; feature = detect impasse/error events (and whether they were resolved) before the assessment item.

title: Coh-Metrix: Analysis of Text on Cohesion and Language (Graesser, McNamara et al., 2004)
relevance: Battery of cohesion/coherence/referential/causal indices; run over student turns to get lexical-sophistication and coherence features that correlate with comprehension/correctness.

title: The Development and Analysis of Tutorial Dialogues in AutoTutor Lite (Graesser et al., 2011, PMC)
relevance: Expectation-misconception tailoring and deep- vs shallow-question effects (deep questions yield larger gains); feature = depth/type of questions posed and expectation-coverage of student answers.

title: Survey of Natural Language Processing for Education: Taxonomy, Systematic Review, and Future Trends (2024)
relevance: Landscape of educational-NLP tasks and methods; a checklist for candidate feature families (assessment, QG, error correction) and modeling patterns to borrow.

title: Predicting Short Response Ratings with Non-Content Related Features: A Hierarchical Modeling Approach (2024)
relevance: Shows non-content signals (length, structure, timing) predict response quality; a near-noise analog arguing to include cheap surface/behavioral features and to model student-level random effects.

title: Survey on Automated Short Answer Grading with Deep Learning (Bonthu et al., 2022)
relevance: Semantic-similarity and neural correctness-scoring methods for free-text answers; supplies features (overlap/embedding similarity to reference) when the transcript contains the student's attempted answer.

title: Studying Alignment in a Collaborative Learning Activity via Automatic Methods (Rajkumar et al., 2021)
relevance: Automatic lexical/syntactic alignment measures predict learning gains; compute student-tutor linguistic convergence over the session as a tutor-agnostic performance signal.

title: Assessing the Efficacy of LLMs in Generating Accurate Teacher Responses (BEA 2023 Shared Task, Baladon et al.)
relevance: Benchmarks what makes a 'good' next tutor turn on the chatroom corpus; the response-quality metrics double as features scoring the tutor turn that precedes each student answer.

title: Learning meets Assessment: On IRT and Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (Deonovic et al., 2018)
relevance: Unifies IRT ability estimation with BKT learning dynamics — supports using a running student-ability and item-difficulty estimate as features.

title: Contextual Slip and Prediction of Student Performance (Baker, Corbett & Aleven, 2008)
relevance: Estimates guess/slip contextually per action rather than as constants — feature idea: predicted slip/guess likelihood from local behavior around a turn.

title: Applications of the Elo Rating System in Adaptive Educational Systems (Pelánek et al., 2016)
relevance: Online Elo skill/difficulty updated per response (with response-time scoring) — a lightweight running ability and item-difficulty feature computable on the fly.

title: A Trainable Spaced Repetition Model for Language Learning / Half-Life Regression (Settles & Meeder, 2016)
relevance: Estimates memory half-life from lag time and prior correct/incorrect counts — yields a recall-probability feature for whether a previously-seen item is retained.

title: Distributed Practice in Verbal Recall Tasks: A Review and Quantitative Synthesis (Cepeda et al., 2006)
relevance: Spacing/lag interacts with retention interval — motivates encoding time-since-last-practice and spacing-gap features (not just counts) as correctness predictors.

title: Enhancing Human Learning via Spaced Repetition Optimization (Tabibian et al., 2019, PNAS)
relevance: Memory-model + optimal review timing (marginal recall gain) — feature idea: expected recall at the moment the next question is posed given review history.

title: Adaptive Forgetting Curves for Spaced Repetition Language Learning (2020)
relevance: Fits per-learner forgetting curves — a personalized decay-rate feature capturing how fast a given student loses a skill between turns.

title: The Right Time to Learn: Mechanisms and Optimization of Spaced Learning (Smolen, Zhang & Byrne, 2016)
relevance: Mechanistic account of spacing effects — justifies nonlinear time-gap transforms (log/exponential) over raw inter-turn intervals as features.

title: Simulated Students in Tutoring Dialogues: Substance or Illusion? (2026)
relevance: Evaluates whether LLM-simulated learner responses reflect real correctness — a caution on synthetic-signal reliability directly relevant to our near-noise setting.

title: Decision Tree Modeling of Wheel-Spinning and Productive Persistence in Skill Builders (Baker et al., 2018)
relevance: Detects unproductive struggle from repeated-attempt patterns — feature idea: recent attempt/error streaks that predict continued failure on the next item.

title: Instruction Based on Self-Explanation (Chi & Wylie / Chi et al. lineage, book chapter)
relevance: Self-explanation predicts deep learning and misconception repair — feature idea: presence and quality of student self-explanation/co-construction turns in the transcript.

title: Litman & Silliman — ITSPOKE: An Intelligent Tutoring Spoken Dialogue System (HLT-NAACL 2004)
relevance: Blueprint pipeline: per student turn, fuse ASR text + acoustic-prosodic + dialogue-history features — the exact feature stack to reuse for next-answer correctness.

title: Forbes-Riley & Litman — Annotating Student Emotional States in Spoken Tutoring Dialogues
relevance: Turn-level affect annotation scheme + lexical/acoustic/dialogue features; gives labelable target states (uncertain/frustrated) usable as engineered features.

title: Pon-Barry & Shieber — Prosodic Manifestations of Confidence and Uncertainty in Spoken Language (Interspeech 2008)
relevance: Answer-level prosodic markers of confidence (falling vs rising contour, latency, fillers) — directly map an answer's prosody to likely correctness.

title: Litman, Forbes-Riley et al. — Prosodic Cues to Disengagement and Uncertainty in Physics Tutorial Dialogues (Interspeech 2012)
relevance: Separates disengagement from uncertainty via prosody; motivates two distinct feature families rather than one 'affect' signal.

title: Litman et al. — Spoken Versus Typed Human and Computer Dialogue Tutoring (IJAIED 2006)
relevance: Shows disfluency/pause counts differ by modality and correlate with learning — supports disfluency-rate features on transcribed speech vs typed baselines.

title: Bevan Jones — The Prosody of Uncertainty for Spoken Dialogue Intelligent Tutoring Systems (thesis)
relevance: End-to-end recipe for extracting and modeling prosodic uncertainty in an ITS; reusable feature-extraction and labeling methodology.

title: Forbes-Riley & Litman — Improving (Meta)Cognitive Tutoring by Detecting and Responding to Uncertainty (AAAI)
relevance: Defines the (in)correct x (un)certain quadrant; the 'correct-but-uncertain' cell argues correctness alone is noisy — add a certainty feature to sharpen prediction.

title: Clark & Fox Tree — Using uh and um in spontaneous speaking (Cognition 2002)
relevance: Treat 'uh' (minor delay) and 'um' (major delay) as separate features, not one filler count — um-rate should be the stronger difficulty/uncertainty signal.

title: Clark & Wasow — Repeating Words in Spontaneous Speech
relevance: Word repeats/restarts index production difficulty; add repetition and self-repair counts as transcript features for planning trouble before an answer.

title: Rohd, et al. — Fillers in Spoken Language Understanding: Computational and Psycholinguistic Perspectives
relevance: Survey of how to encode fillers for NLP models; guides whether to keep, tag, or mask 'um/uh' tokens when feeding transcripts to a classifier.

title: Barisic et al. — Cognitive Load Increases Spoken and Gestural Hesitation Frequency (Languages 2023)
relevance: Empirical link hesitation-rate to cognitive load; justifies per-turn hesitation frequency as a load proxy predicting an impending wrong answer.

title: Automatic Disfluency Detection from Untranscribed Speech
relevance: Lets you compute disfluency-rate features straight from audio even when the transcript is clean/normalized — recovers a signal often stripped by ASR.

title: What makes a good pause? Investigating the turn-holding effects of fillers
relevance: Distinguishes turn-holding vs hesitation pauses; feature idea: pause duration x position (mid-answer vs turn-final) rather than raw silence totals.

title: Integrating Pause Information with Word Embeddings in Language Models (AD detection)
relevance: Directly shows how to inject pause tokens/durations into word embeddings for an LM — a concrete architecture for a prosody-aware transcript model.

title: Goupil et al. — Listeners' perceptions of certainty and honesty share a common prosodic signature (Nature Communications 2020)
relevance: Identifies the canonical prosodic signature (intonation+loudness+duration) of certainty; a compact, theory-grounded acoustic feature vector to extract.

title: Lubold, Walker & Pon-Barry — Automated Pitch Convergence Improves Learning in a Social Teachable Robot (AIED 2018)
relevance: Causal evidence that pitch entrainment aids learning; compute tutor-student pitch/rate convergence over the window as a rapport/engagement feature.

title: Levitan — Acoustic-Prosodic Entrainment in Human-Human and Human-Computer Dialogue (thesis)
relevance: Catalog of concrete entrainment metrics (proximity, convergence, synchrony) with formulas — ready-to-implement paraverbal alignment features.

title: Predicting affective engagement and mental strain from prosodic speech features (PMC)
relevance: Maps prosodic features to engagement and mental strain; supplies a validated prosody-to-engagement regression target for the tutoring setting.

title: Automatic Detection of a Student's Affective States for Intelligent Teaching Systems (PMC)
relevance: Sensor-free affect detection from interaction logs/dialogue; shows text-only affect features are feasible when audio is unavailable.

title: Utilizing NLP for Automated Assessment of Classroom Discussion
relevance: Automated discussion-quality scoring from transcripts; yields discourse-quality features (questioning, elaboration) as covariates.

title: Mostow & Duong — Automated Assessment of Oral Reading Prosody (AIED 2009)
relevance: Correlate a student's pitch/intensity/pause/word-duration contour against a fluent template; contour-similarity score predicts comprehension gains.

title: Chen et al. — Automated Scoring of Nonnative Speech Using SpeechRater v5.0 (ETS, open access)
relevance: Production-grade catalog of fluency/prosody/pronunciation features from spoken responses — an off-the-shelf feature dictionary to mine transcripts+audio.

title: A Survey of Deep Learning-Based Multimodal Emotion Recognition: Speech, Text, and Face (Entropy 2023)
relevance: Names extraction toolkits (openSMILE, COVAREP) and fusion architectures for combining acoustic+text emotion features into the model.

title: The BEA 2023 Shared Task on Generating AI Teacher Responses in Educational Dialogues
relevance: Benchmark + metrics for tutor-response quality; supplies evaluation axes and baselines you can repurpose as response-quality features.

title: Improving Interpretability of Deep Sequential KT with Question-centric Cognitive Representations (QIKT)
relevance: Fine-grained, question-sensitive KC mastery estimates; suggests per-question cognitive-representation features rather than a single scalar ability.

title: Is ChatGPT a Good Teacher Coach? Zero-Shot Scoring of Classroom Instruction (Wang & Demszky, BEA 2023)
relevance: LLM zero-shot scoring of transcript segments against observation rubrics; a cheap way to generate instructional-quality features per segment.

title: Edu-ConvoKit: An Open-Source Library for Education Conversation Data
relevance: Ready tooling to compute talk-time, question rate, uptake, and talk-move features from transcripts; practical feature-extraction backbone.

title: Automated Evaluation of Classroom Instructional Support with LLMs and BoWs
relevance: Connects global instructional-support prediction to specific utterances; hybrid BoW+LLM features that are interpretable and cheap for near-noise settings.

title: Stepwise Verification and Remediation of Student Reasoning Errors with LLM Tutors
relevance: Per-step verification of student reasoning; a running 'error detected at step k' signal that anticipates failure on the next assessment item.

title: Self-Explanation of Worked Examples Integrated in an Intelligent Tutoring System
relevance: Shows eliciting (high-quality) self-explanation drives gains; feature: count/quality of student self-explanation and self-evaluation turns in the transcript.

title: What Impacts Should We Expect from Tutoring at Scale? Exploring Meta-Analytic Generalizability (Kraft, Schueler & Falken, 2024)
relevance: Effect sizes shrink sharply with scale/target-equivalence -> calibrate expectations for a near-noise task and regularize the model's baseline learning-gain prior.

title: Educational Outcomes of Tutoring: A Meta-analysis of Findings (Cohen, Kulik & Kulik, 1982)
relevance: Domain-specific gaps (math d=0.60 vs reading d=0.29) argue for subject-interaction features or separate per-domain correctness models.

title: A Meta-Analysis of the Effectiveness of Intelligent Tutoring Systems on K-12 Students' Mathematical Learning (Steenbergen-Hu & Cooper, 2013)
relevance: Grade/subject moderators specific to math ITS -> grade-band and math-topic moderators for math next-item correctness prediction.

title: Effectiveness of Mastery Learning Programs: A Meta-Analysis (Kulik, Kulik & Bangert-Drowns, 1990)
relevance: Gains largest for weaker students (d=0.61) -> interaction feature prior-ability x amount-of-mastery-practice in the session.

title: A Blueprint for Scaling Tutoring and Mentoring Across Public Schools (Kraft & Falken, 2021)
relevance: Names the design levers (dosage, small group, tutor continuity, relationship) -> session-structure features: group size, cadence, same-tutor-continuity flag.

title: Design Principles for Accelerating Student Learning with High-Impact Tutoring (EdResearch for Action, 2024)
relevance: Curriculum-aligned, data-informed tutoring -> feature: alignment between the session's covered KCs and the upcoming assessment's KCs.

title: One to One Tuition - Teaching and Learning Toolkit (Education Endowment Foundation)
relevance: Group size (1:1 vs 2-3) moderates impact -> simple format indicator feature (one-to-one vs small-group) with 'smaller = better' prior.

title: Knowledge Tracing: Modeling the Acquisition of Procedural Knowledge (Corbett & Anderson, 1995)
relevance: BKT HMM with prior/learn/slip/guess -> the canonical baseline: per-KC latent mastery probability is the primary predictor of next-item correctness.

title: Eliciting Self-Explanations Improves Understanding (Chi, de Leeuw, Chiu & LaVancher, 1994)
relevance: Student-generated self-explanations predict understanding gains -> feature: count/quality of student self-explanation utterances in the transcript.

title: The Power of Feedback (Hattie & Timperley, 2007)
relevance: Feedback operates at task/process/self-regulation/self levels with different efficacy -> categorize each tutor feedback turn by level as a predictor.

title: Test-Enhanced Learning: Taking Memory Tests Improves Long-Term Retention (Roediger & Karpicke, 2006)
relevance: Retrieval/testing effect -> feature: number of prior retrieval attempts and prior successful retrievals of the target KC in the session.

title: Exploring the Assistance Dilemma in Experiments with Cognitive Tutors (Koedinger & Aleven, 2007)
relevance: Help amount/timing tradeoff and bottom-out-hint abuse -> features: hint depth used, help-requested-before-attempt, bottom-out-hint rate.
